# CNS — Active Learning Activities
### Cryptography and Network Security | BETIT16326

| **Class** | C |

---

## Overview

This is a single-file interactive web application built with **HTML, CSS, and JavaScript** that demonstrates three cryptographic concepts through live, hands-on simulations. All cryptographic operations run entirely in the browser using the native **Web Crypto API** — no backend, no external libraries, no internet required after loading.

The UI uses a **dark cyberpunk / terminal aesthetic** with phosphor-green styling, scanline overlays, glitch animations, and monospace fonts (Orbitron + Share Tech Mono).

---

## Screenshots

### 🏠 Home Screen
![Home Screen](data:image/png;base64,)

The landing page displays student info and three ALA cards. Each card navigates to a dedicated interactive module.

---

### ALA-01 — Digital Signature System
![Digital Signature System](data:image/png;base64,)

Demonstrates RSA-2048 digital signature generation and verification using SHA-256 hashing.

---

### ALA-02 — Integrity Analyzer
![Integrity Analyzer](data:image/png;base64,)

Compares SHA-1, SHA-256, and SHA-512 hash outputs and visualises the avalanche effect with a bit-flip grid.

---

### ALA-03 — HMAC Authentication
![HMAC Authentication](data:image/png;base64,)

Simulates a sender-receiver HMAC-SHA256 authentication flow with tamper detection.

---

## Features

### ALA-01 · Digital Signature System
- Generates a real **2048-bit RSA key pair** via `crypto.subtle.generateKey`
- Computes **SHA-256 hash** of the input message
- Signs the message with the **private key** using `RSASSA-PKCS1-v1_5`
- Verifies the signature with the **public key**
- **Tamper Test** — appends `[TAMPERED]` to the message and shows signature failure

### ALA-02 · Integrity Analyzer
- Computes **SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512** hashes for two input messages side by side
- Displays a **Hash Comparison Table** with all digests
- Renders an **Avalanche Effect progress bar** showing % of bits flipped between hashes
- Renders a **256-bit flip grid** — red cells = flipped bits, dark cells = unchanged bits

### ALA-03 · HMAC Authentication
- Sender inputs a **shared secret key** and message, generates an **HMAC-SHA256 tag**
- Tag is "transmitted" to the Receiver panel
- Receiver **recomputes HMAC** using the same key and compares
- **Test Tampered** mode modifies the message to demonstrate MAC mismatch and tamper detection

---

## Tech Stack

| Technology | Usage |
|---|---|
| HTML5 | Structure and layout |
| CSS3 | Cyberpunk theme, animations, scanline effect, grid background |
| Vanilla JavaScript | All logic and DOM manipulation |
| Web Crypto API | RSA key generation, SHA hashing, HMAC signing |
| Google Fonts | Orbitron (display), Share Tech Mono (monospace), Rajdhani (body) |

**No frameworks. No build tools. No dependencies. Open the `.html` file and it works.**

---

## How to Run

```bash
# No installation needed.
# Just open the file in any modern browser:

open CNS_ALA.html          # macOS
start CNS_ALA.html         # Windows
xdg-open CNS_ALA.html      # Linux
```

> Requires a browser that supports the **Web Crypto API** (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari — all modern versions).

---

## File Structure

```
CNS_ALA.html      ← Entire application (HTML + CSS + JS in one file)
README.md         ← This file (images fully embedded, works standalone)
```

---

## Cryptographic Concepts Covered

**Digital Signature** — Provides authentication, integrity, and non-repudiation. Message → SHA-256 hash → encrypted with private key → signature. Receiver decrypts with public key and compares hashes.

**Avalanche Effect** — A core property of cryptographic hash functions where a single-bit change in input causes approximately 50% of output bits to flip, making hash outputs completely unpredictable.

**HMAC** — Hash-based Message Authentication Code. Uses a shared secret key + SHA-256 to produce an authentication tag. Proves both integrity (message unchanged) and authenticity (only key-holders can generate valid tags).

---
